---
title: Building a Reproducible Analytical Pipeline (RAP) for Simulation with Python and R
author: Amy Heather
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "DES RAP Book: <https://pythonhealthdatascience.github.io/des_rap_book/>"
    include-in-header:
      - text: <script src="javascript/incremental-show-first.js"></script>
---

## Reproducibility {auto-animate=true}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=65%}

Reproducibility is the ability to regenerate results (e.g. tables, figures) using the provided code and data.

:::

::::

![](images/reproducibility.png)

## Reproducibility {auto-animate=true}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=65%}

Reproducibility is the ability to regenerate results (e.g. tables, figures) using the provided code and data.

:::

::: {.column width=35%}

![](images/reproducibility.png)

:::

::::

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=20%}

Why?

:::

::: {.column width=80%}

::: {.incremental .show-first}
* Reuse your own work more easily.
* Improve code clarity and quality
* Save time when revisiting analyses.
* Build trust.
* Help others verify and reuse your work.
:::

:::

::::

## Reproducible Analytical Pipelines

**RAP**: Systematic approach. Every step (end-to-end) is transparent, automated and repeatable.

![](images/rap.png)

## Discrete-event simulation (DES)

DES model a system as a sequence of events. For example, patients arriving, waiting, receiving treatment, and leaving.

![](videos/vidigi_des.gif)

## Discrete-event simulation (DES) {auto-animate=true}

DES incorporate uncertainty - instead of e.g., fixed frequency of arrivals, sample from statistical distribution.

![](images/samples.png)

## Discrete-event simulation (DES) {auto-animate=true}

DES incorporate uncertainty - instead of e.g., fixed frequency of arrivals, sample from statistical distribution.

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width=50%}

![](images/samples.png)

::: {style="text-align: right;"}

<br>

This means each run <br>
produces different results.

:::

:::

::: {.column width=50%}

![](images/varying_outputs.png)

:::

::::

## How to construct DES as RAP?

**Resource 1: NHS Levels of RAP**

<iframe width="100%" height="500" src="https://nhsdigital.github.io/rap-community-of-practice/introduction_to_RAP/levels_of_RAP/" title="NHS RAP Community of Practice: Levels of RAP"></iframe>

## How to construct DES as RAP?

**Resource 2: DES reproducibility recommendations**

<embed src="pdf/jos_reproducibility_paper.pdf" width="100%" height="500" />

## Consolidated criteria

Grid? Diagram? That aligns with pages.

## Book/Site

We produced a book... show book... presentation... top level overview of what we cover etc... go to book for full thing...

## Version control

TODO

## Environments

TODO
